title: fuy.gg
body:
# Fuy\.gg
Fuy\.gg aims to be a successor and upgrade to AvoMod, fixing many of the long-standing issues that have plagued AvoMod's 1.19 port. 

Mainly fixing:
1. Reveal Nicknames
2. Stack Duplicate Messages
3. Commands such as /am find, pg, om, and ls being affected by the name change glitch

## Territory Helper Menu
### A feature-rich overhaul of the regular Wynncraft territory menu

- Smooth Scrolling
- Extensive search functionality
- Displays the acronym for each territory
- Highlights territories based on multiple different filters
- Tallies all the territories that match each filter


![Territory Helper Menu](https://i.imgur.com/V5Fk7rk.png)

# War QoL

* Timer Overlay
   * Displays all timers sorted from soonest to latest
   * Highlights the territory you're in
   * Display the defense of the territory
   * Highlights defenses that are from the Guild Map
* Shows beacons of all timers
   * Labels the distance and territory
   * Colored by whether they are
      * The soonest timer
      * Timers you queued
      * Regular Timers
 * War Info Overlay:
      * Displays remaining tower EHP
      * Tower DPS
      * Team DPS over 1s, 5s, and the entire war
      * Estimated Time Remaining
 * Weekly War Count Overlay
 * Display Tower Stats after war

# Miscellaneous Features

* Server Info Overlay
  * Displays uptime for the newest world & the current world
  * Only shows when holding tab
* Bomb Bell Overlay
* Auto /stream
* Click To Congratulate 
* Nickname Revealer
* Duplicate Message Stacking    
* Guild Bank Keybind

# Commands

* Configs command (Opens up the config menu)
   * /fuy config
* Auto /stream command (toggles autostream)
   * /fuy autostream
* Online Members command (displays all online members of a guild)
   * /fuy onlinemembers <name or prefix>
* Find command (finds the world of a player)
   * /fuy find <ign or uuid>
* Last Seen command (displays the how long ago the player last logged in)
   * /fuy lastseen <ign or uuid>
* Player Guild command (displays the guild of a player)
   * /fuy playerguild <ign or uuid>
* Wars command (counts the number of wars in the specified timeframe)
   * /fuy wars <duration (ex. 7d)>
